data.body:
ROUND 3 — corrections + relaxed-constraint research (3 subagents) for the 6f2d brainstorm.

LANGGRAPH CORRECTION (confirmed in code + docs): two layers, don't conflate. (a) AGENT-STEP runtime
IS a thin layer over an established lib: LangGraphRunner uses langchain.agents.create_agent + ToolStrategy
(runner.py:124-169), optional behind [agents]. Chosen for NATIVE Langfuse tracing (every other agent
framework needs OTel) — after a research spike + 2 Opus design reviews (docs/llm-framework.md). (b) The
WORKFLOW ORCHESTRATION spine is homegrown (stdlib graphlib topo-sort), deliberately NOT
LangGraph-as-engine / Temporal / Prefect / Burr. So Joe is right: LLM execution rides on LangChain/LangGraph;
only the thin DAG orchestrator is ours.

DESIGN-RATIONALE DIG: the "why" Joe remembered EXISTS, but there is NO dedicated a88f design *session log*
— the rationale lives in: docs/llm-framework.md (LangGraph-for-Langfuse + 2 Opus reviews), the a88f stories
(WS-C Burr tripwire; WS-I read-only-render deferral "no round-trip risk", no user sign-off; WS-F Langfuse
trace/replica-only; WS-K retire LangflowRunner stub), docs/architecture.md, and the 6f2d description. (A
subagent circularly cited THIS session's log ad3f as "the design log" — discount that.) KEY: the Burr
"tripwire" CI test names the 4 adoption triggers = {non-linear branching, dynamic fan-out, parallelism,
durable-pause}; executor state pre-shaped toward Burr's immutable State for a cheap migration.

CRITICAL CONNECTION: Joe's invariant I1 (human must edit LOOPS + CONDITIONAL GATES) trips the Burr
triggers (branching + loops). So the visual-edit requirement FORCES the executor to change regardless of
format — extend the homegrown executor, adopt Burr, or inherit a format-native engine.

VIABLE SHORTLIST under RELAXED constraints (diff-noise OK; heavy edit-deps OK; light runtime; LLM-editable):
1. BPMN — Camunda Modeler (MIT desktop, no watermark, v5.48 2026-06; edits local .bpmn = source of truth)
   or bpmn-js (watermark) + SpiffWorkflow (LGPL, pure-Python, one dep lxml, v3.1.2) OR a DIY xml.etree
   interpreter. Expresses XOR/OR/AND gateways, loops, multiInstance fan-out, scriptTask vs serviceTask,
   legal <rebar:*> extension elements. The ONLY family that needs NEITHER a new editor NOR a new engine.
   GOTCHAS: (a) LLMs are BAD at hand-editing raw BPMN XML — hallucinate IDs, verbose (BPMN Assistant
   arXiv 2509.24592: routing edits via a JSON IR cut failures 11->2, ~75% fewer tokens) => edit a compact
   IR, serialize to BPMN. (b) DI/layout co-mingled with semantics in ONE file -> a semantic node w/o a
   BPMNShape opens INVISIBLE, silently; bpmn-auto-layout is all-or-nothing (clobbers hand layout). (c)
   custom <rebar:*> elements need a REGISTERED moddle descriptor or they're stripped silently on save.
   (d) XSD validates ~54% of constraints, 0% of references -> need a bpmnlint-style semantic gate. (e)
   BPMN's enterprise heaviness clashes with rebar's lean GH-Actions aesthetic.
2. draw.io + custom shape library over rebar's OWN IR — drawio-desktop (Apache-2.0, offline, v30 2026-06),
   uncompressed XML, custom shapes + <object> metadata round-trip, official drawio-mcp for agent edits.
   Keep rebar's lean executor (EXTENDED for loops/branch/fan-out = the Burr moment). RISK: draw.io enforces
   NO schema -> rebar owns the whole drawio<->IR validator + mapping; possible two-artifact (YAML + .drawio)
   sync. Keeps agents on our friendly format; no JS/engine lock-in.
3. ASL (Amazon States Language) — mature JSON spec (Choice/Map/loops), off-the-shelf bidirectional editor =
   AWS Toolkit for VS Code Workflow Studio (Apache-2.0, offline on local .asl.json). Agent-friendlier than
   XML; schema-validatable (asl-validator/statelint). COSTS: build an ~8-state Python interpreter (no live
   OSS Python runtime); editor is VS-Code-bound; no native LLM step (Task-as-agent-step mapping).

REJECTED/traps (cited): XState/Stately (JS-locked, SaaS editor, dead xstate-python), SCXML (no good OSS
editor; python-statemachine has an SCXML reader but editors are Windows-only/IDE-bound/0-star), Node-RED
(JS-coupled, no Python exec, minified flows.json), litegraph.js (more glue, Canvas2D, quiet), react-flow/
rete/drawflow (build-your-own libraries = the non-goal), Langflow/n8n/Flowise/Dify/Kestra/Windmill
(DB-canonical, breaks file-source-of-truth), LangGraph Studio (code-canonical, read-only debugger),
promptflow (deprecated 2027, no loops), MS Conductor / Oracle WayFlow (file+Python but NO visual editor yet).

CORE TENSION (flag to Joe): the formats best for HUMAN visual editing (BPMN/draw.io XML) are the WORST for
LLM editing (I2); the agent-friendliest format (our YAML/JSON IR) has no off-the-shelf editor. => points
toward a DUAL-representation design (clean rebar IR as the agent+execution source of truth + a round-tripped
visual format for the editor), OR ASL-JSON as the single-format compromise. NOTE: novelty check — all three
shortlist options are PROVEN OSS patterns, not novel; BPMN is the most-trodden + standards-based.
